title: Mathematics for AI
description: Hand-written closed questions made file by file from the Math AI Markdown sources.

questions.prompt: What is the purpose of checking worked solutions after attempting tutorial questions?
choices: It explains the reasoning after the learner tries the exercise
To make the student explore before seeing formal guidance
To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
To build understanding before seeing the solution
questions.explanation: Solutions are useful after attempting the work because they show the correct reasoning path.

questions.prompt: Why does the tutorial ask students to attempt questions before checking answers?
choices: To build understanding before seeing the solution
To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
It explains the reasoning after the learner tries the exercise
To make the student explore before seeing formal guidance
questions.explanation: Active attempt makes the solution more meaningful.

questions.prompt: What is the role of a solution workbook in active learning?
choices: It explains the reasoning after the learner tries the exercise
To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
To build understanding before seeing the solution
To make the student explore before seeing formal guidance
questions.explanation: The workbook supports guided practice and feedback.

questions.prompt: What is the purpose of the 'Try this first' structure?
choices: It explains the reasoning after the learner tries the exercise
To make the student explore before seeing formal guidance
To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
To build understanding before seeing the solution
questions.explanation: The workbook is designed around active-learning attempts before hints or answers.

questions.prompt: Which mathematical foundation is strongly connected to systems of linear equations?
choices: To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
To build understanding before seeing the solution
It explains the reasoning after the learner tries the exercise
Linear algebra
questions.explanation: Systems of linear equations are a central topic in linear algebra.

questions.prompt: Which object represents an ordered list of numbers?
choices: To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
To build understanding before seeing the solution
Vector
Matrix
questions.explanation: A vector stores several components in an ordered form.

questions.prompt: Which object is arranged in rows and columns?
choices: To build understanding before seeing the solution
Matrix
Vector
To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
questions.explanation: A matrix is a rectangular array of values.

questions.prompt: When is matrix multiplication AB defined?
choices: It explains the reasoning after the learner tries the exercise
When the number of columns of A equals the number of rows of B
To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
To build understanding before seeing the solution
questions.explanation: The inner dimensions must match for matrix multiplication.

questions.prompt: What is Gaussian elimination used for?
choices: Solving systems of linear equations
To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
To build understanding before seeing the solution
It explains the reasoning after the learner tries the exercise
questions.explanation: Elimination transforms equations to a simpler form for solving.

questions.prompt: What must a subspace be closed under?
choices: To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
To build understanding before seeing the solution
It explains the reasoning after the learner tries the exercise
Vector addition and scalar multiplication
questions.explanation: Closure under addition and scalar multiplication is essential for subspaces.

exams.title: W2-P2 Linear Independence, Basis, and Dimension

questions.prompt: What does a zero determinant indicate in the lecture notes?
choices: The matrix is invertible
To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
To build understanding before seeing the solution
Dimension collapse or rank deficiency
questions.explanation: The Markdown notes connect zero determinant with dimension collapse.

questions.prompt: What does full rank usually imply for a square matrix?
choices: To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
To build understanding before seeing the solution
The matrix is invertible
Dimension collapse or rank deficiency
questions.explanation: For a square matrix, full rank means the columns are independent and the matrix is invertible.

exams.title: W2-P3 Linear Mappings, Rank, and Null Space

questions.prompt: In Row Echelon Form, where must all-zero rows appear?
choices: Each pivot appears to the right of the pivot above it
To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
To build understanding before seeing the solution
Below all nonzero rows
questions.explanation: The notes list zero rows below nonzero rows as a REF condition.

questions.prompt: What creates the staircase pattern in Row Echelon Form?
choices: To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
To build understanding before seeing the solution
Each pivot appears to the right of the pivot above it
Below all nonzero rows
questions.explanation: The pivots move right as you go down the rows.

exams.title: W3-P1 Norms, Inner Products, and Distance

questions.prompt: What does a norm measure?
choices: It explains the reasoning after the learner tries the exercise
The length or magnitude of a vector
To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
To build understanding before seeing the solution
questions.explanation: Norms quantify vector size.

questions.prompt: What does it mean for two vectors to be orthogonal?
choices: To build understanding before seeing the solution
It explains the reasoning after the learner tries the exercise
Their inner product is zero
To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
questions.explanation: Orthogonality is expressed by a zero inner product.

questions.prompt: What is Gram-Schmidt used to construct?
choices: An orthogonal or orthonormal set of vectors
To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
To build understanding before seeing the solution
It explains the reasoning after the learner tries the exercise
questions.explanation: Gram-Schmidt turns independent vectors into an orthogonal basis.

questions.prompt: What does a determinant represent geometrically?
choices: To build understanding before seeing the solution
It explains the reasoning after the learner tries the exercise
Volume or area scaling by a linear transformation
To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
questions.explanation: Determinants measure scaling and reveal singularity.

exams.title: W4-P2 Diagonalization and Change of Basis

questions.prompt: Why is diagonalization useful?
choices: To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
To build understanding before seeing the solution
It explains the reasoning after the learner tries the exercise
It can simplify repeated matrix operations
questions.explanation: A diagonal form is easier to work with for powers and transformations.

exams.title: W4-P3 Quadratic Forms and Positive Definiteness

questions.prompt: What does covariance measure?
choices: It explains the reasoning after the learner tries the exercise
How two variables vary together
To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
To build understanding before seeing the solution
questions.explanation: The notes use study hours and exam scores to show joint variation.

questions.prompt: What is the general purpose of matrix decomposition?
choices: It explains the reasoning after the learner tries the exercise
Factor a matrix into simpler matrices for analysis or computation
To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
To build understanding before seeing the solution
questions.explanation: Decompositions expose structure and simplify computations.

questions.prompt: What does SVD decompose a matrix into?
choices: To build understanding before seeing the solution
It explains the reasoning after the learner tries the exercise
Singular vectors and singular values
To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
questions.explanation: SVD represents a matrix through directions and scaling strengths.

exams.title: W7-P1 Derivatives, Gradients, and Taylor Ideas

questions.prompt: What does a gradient indicate?
choices: The direction of steepest increase of a function
To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
To build understanding before seeing the solution
It explains the reasoning after the learner tries the exercise
questions.explanation: Gradients guide optimization algorithms in AI.

exams.title: W7-P2 Jacobians, Hessians, and Chain Rule

questions.prompt: What does the chain rule help compute?
choices: It explains the reasoning after the learner tries the exercise
Derivatives of composite functions
To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
To build understanding before seeing the solution
questions.explanation: The chain rule is essential for gradients through layered models.

questions.prompt: What is hypothesis testing used for?
choices: Making decisions about a population parameter using sample data
To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
To build understanding before seeing the solution
It explains the reasoning after the learner tries the exercise
questions.explanation: Hypothesis testing supports statistical decisions from samples.

questions.prompt: What does a Z-test require in the lecture formula?
choices: To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
To build understanding before seeing the solution
It explains the reasoning after the learner tries the exercise
Sample mean, claimed population mean, population standard deviation, and sample size
questions.explanation: The Z statistic compares the sample mean to a hypothesized mean using standard error.

questions.prompt: What is an optimization objective in machine learning?
choices: To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
To build understanding before seeing the solution
It explains the reasoning after the learner tries the exercise
A function the model tries to minimize or maximize
questions.explanation: Learning usually means optimizing an objective such as loss.

exams.title: W9-P2 Gradient Descent and Learning Rates

questions.prompt: What does the learning rate control in gradient descent?
choices: To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
To build understanding before seeing the solution
It explains the reasoning after the learner tries the exercise
The step size of parameter updates
questions.explanation: A learning rate that is too large or too small can hurt convergence.

questions.prompt: Why is discrete mathematics useful in AI?
choices: It models objects, rules, relationships, paths, and combinations
To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
To build understanding before seeing the solution
It explains the reasoning after the learner tries the exercise
questions.explanation: The notes explain that many AI problems involve discrete structures.

questions.prompt: What are the two main parts of a graph?
choices: To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
To build understanding before seeing the solution
It explains the reasoning after the learner tries the exercise
Vertices and edges
questions.explanation: A graph represents objects as vertices and relationships as edges.

exams.title: W11-P1 Numerical Stability and Error Analysis

questions.prompt: Why can computers introduce numerical error?
choices: It explains the reasoning after the learner tries the exercise
They represent real numbers using finite precision
To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
To build understanding before seeing the solution
questions.explanation: Finite precision means real values are approximated.

questions.prompt: What is overflow?
choices: A number is too large for the computer to represent
A number is too close to zero to represent accurately
To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
To build understanding before seeing the solution
questions.explanation: Overflow happens when a value exceeds the representable range.

questions.prompt: What is underflow?
choices: A number is too large for the computer to represent
To compare reasoning steps and correct mistakes
To build understanding before seeing the solution
A number is too close to zero to represent accurately
questions.explanation: Very tiny values may be rounded to zero.
